title: CIL Industrial Engineering — Advanced Mock 14 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.solution: E

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of CATALYST:
questions.solution: Barrier

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He asked (A) / that whether (B) / I could (C) / help him. (D)
questions.solution: B - remove 'that' or 'whether'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of FACILITATE:
questions.solution: Hinder

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The price of (A) / these goods (B) / have increased (C) / significantly. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'has increased'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of LACONIC:
questions.solution: Verbose

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The committee (A) / have submitted (B) / its report (C) / to the board. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'has submitted'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ALLEVIATE:
questions.solution: Aggravate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The commitee (A) / has given (B) / their report (C) / on time. (D)
questions.solution: A - should be 'committee'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of BENEVOLENCE:
questions.solution: Malevolence

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / more cleverer (B) / than his (C) / brother. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'cleverer' or 'more clever'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of BELLIGERENT:
questions.solution: Peaceful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The furniture (A) / in this room (B) / are very (C) / expensive. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'is very'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of LETHARGIC:
questions.solution: Energetic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: I have (A) / been working (B) / in this organisation (C) / since ten years. (D)
questions.solution: D - should be 'for ten years'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of CONUNDRUM:
questions.solution: Solution

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Neither he (A) / nor his brother (B) / are going (C) / to the party. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'is going'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of VENERATE:
questions.solution: Despise

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To cut corners':
options: To do something cheaply or easily
questions.solution: To do something cheaply or easily

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I am looking forward to meet you.
options: I am looking forward for meeting you.
I look forward to meet you.
I am looking forward to meeting you.
I am looking forward to meet you.
questions.solution: I am looking forward to meeting you.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He told the news to his brother and I.
options: He told the news to I and my brother.
He told the news to his brother and me.
He told the news to his brother and I.
He told the news to my brother and myself.
questions.solution: He told the news to his brother and me.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'A blessing in disguise':
options: A good thing that seemed bad
questions.solution: A good thing that seemed bad

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Each of the participants were given a certificate.
options: Every of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants was given a certificate.
Each of the participant was given a certificate.
questions.solution: Each of the participants was given a certificate.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'The ball is in your court':
options: It is your turn to act
questions.solution: It is your turn to act

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ARDENT:
questions.solution: Enthusiastic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of OBSTINATE:
questions.solution: Flexible

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 54 min, 5 stations and a cycle time of 10 min. Its balance efficiency is:
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σt/(N·C) = 54/(5×10) = 108%.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Project crashing: shortening schedule by:
options: Adding resources to critical path activities
questions.solution: Crashing: add resources to critical activities. Compare cost of crashing vs savings. Crash lowest cost per time unit first.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability of series system:
options: R_s = R₁ × R₂ × ... × R_n. Product of individual reliabilities
questions.solution: Series: if any component fails → system fails. Parallel (redundant): R_p = 1 - Π(1-R_i).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Fair Share Allocation: distributes limited inventory:
questions.solution: Fair share: allocates available inventory proportionally to all demands. Ensures equitable distribution across customers/products.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 40, lead time = 6 days, safety stock = 50 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 40×6 + 50 = 290 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.95 and 0.8 are in series. The system reliability is:
questions.solution: Series: Rs = R₁×R₂ = 0.95×0.8 = 0.76.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Time study rating factor adjusts:
questions.solution: Rating: pace relative to normal (100%). Standard time = observed * rating * (1 + allowance).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹50/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:
questions.solution: Q_BE = FC/(p − v) = 50000/(50 − 50) = Infinity units.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Sensitivity analysis in capital budgeting varies:
options: One input at a time while holding others constant
questions.solution: Sensitivity: change one variable, see effect on NPV/IRR. Identifies critical variables. Spider diagram visualizes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.5, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 280, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Automated guided vehicle — path conflict:
options: Zone control vs collision avoidance. Bidirectional path. Traffic management algorithm
questions.solution: AGV conflict: zone blocking. Path reservation. Dynamic rerouting. Free-range AGVs (LiDAR).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: REBA (Rapid Entire Body Assessment):
options: Whole body assessment. Score 1-4: low risk. 5-7: medium. 8+: high risk
questions.solution: REBA: Hignett & McAtamney. Trunk, neck, legs, arms, wrists. Activity and coupling scores.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Throughput Accounting: throughput is:
options: Sales revenue minus totally variable costs
questions.solution: T = Sales - TVC (materials, commissions). Not traditional accounting. Focus on cash generation, not allocation.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Kano model classifies product features as:
questions.solution: Kano: Must-be (expected), One-dimensional (more=better), Attractive (delighters). Change over time (attractive becomes must-be).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FMECA (Failure Mode Effects & Criticality Analysis):
questions.solution: FMECA: criticality matrix. Failure mode probability level (A to E). Severity class (I to IV).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Capacity Requirements Planning (CRP) uses:
questions.solution: CRP: detailed capacity from MRP. Uses routings, setup/run times, work center loads. Follows MRP run.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Six Sigma process targets a defect level of about:
questions.solution: Six Sigma (with 1.5σ shift) corresponds to ~3.4 DPMO.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ergonomics — NIOSH lifting equation:
options: RWL = LC × HM × VM × DM × AM × FM × CM. RWL ≤ 23 kg (LC). LI = load weight / RWL
questions.solution: NIOSH: Lifting Index > 1 = hazardous. Multiplier for horizontal, vertical, distance, asymmetry. LI < 1 = ok.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Branch and cut (B&C):
options: Branch & bound + cutting planes. Gomory fractional cuts, mixed integer rounding, cover cuts. Used in CPLEX, Gurobi
questions.solution: B&C: cuts tighten LP relaxation. Reduce branching. Preprocessing (presolve) also critical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — benchmarking:
options: Compare with best-in-class. Metrics: cost, quality, time, features. Identify improvement gap
questions.solution: Benchmarking: competitive, functional, generic. Reverse engineering. Best practices transfer.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Line balancing: goal is to:
questions.solution: Line balancing: assign tasks to stations. Balance delay = idle time. Maximize efficiency = useful time / available time.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Operations chart analyzes:
options: Body movements in manual work tasks
questions.solution: Operations chart: symbols for transport, delay, storage, inspection, operation.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Input modeling: goodness-of-fit test determines:
options: Which distribution best fits historical data
questions.solution: Goodness-of-fit: chi-square, KS, Anderson-Darling tests. Compare data to candidate distributions. Select best.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CRAFT algorithm: departments must have:
options: Equal area or adjacent departments to swap
questions.solution: CRAFT: swaps adjacent departments of equal area, or any two departments with cost calculation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Green supply chain management:
options: Reverse logistics, remanufacturing, carbon footprint reduction. ISO 14000, circular economy
questions.solution: Green: eco-design, green purchasing. Emissions tracking: Scope 1, 2, 3. Closed-loop SC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 10 days/₹4000 and crash time/cost are 5 days/₹5000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (5000−4000)/(10−5) = ₹200/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Box-Cox transformation:
options: Power transform to make data normal. Y' = (Y^λ - 1)/λ. λ estimated by MLE
questions.solution: Box-Cox: λ = 0 → log. λ = 0.5 → square root. λ = 1 → no transform. Normalize data.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 1000 units, ordering cost = ₹100/order, holding cost = ₹2/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.8 and 0.85 are in parallel. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has a cycle time of 4 min and runs for 480 min/shift. The shift output is:
questions.solution: Output = available time / cycle time = 480/4 = 120 units.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Work measurement primary purpose:
options: Determine time for qualified worker to perform task
questions.solution: Work measurement: establish standard time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The break-even quantity equals fixed cost divided by the:
options: Contribution margin per unit (price − variable cost)
questions.solution: Q_BE = FC/(p − v); each unit's contribution covers fixed cost until break-even.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rough-cut capacity planning (RCCP):
options: CPOF (capacity planning using overall factors), BOM-based (capacity bill), RP (resource profile)
questions.solution: RCCP: validates MPS feasibility. Resource profile: time-phased. Monthly or weekly check.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ROP: demand=100/day, lead time=5 days, safety stock=33. ROP=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bullwhip effect: the variance of orders is typically:
options: Greater than variance of demand (amplification upstream)
questions.solution: Bullwhip: Var(orders) > Var(demand). Increases upstream. VMI, information sharing reduce it.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supply chain coordination — contract types:
options: Buyback (returns), revenue sharing, quantity flexibility, profit sharing. Align incentives
questions.solution: Contracts: risk-sharing. Buyback: for fashion (high uncertainty). Revenue sharing: video rental.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contract manufacturing / outsourcing benefits:
options: Focus on core competency, reduce capital investment, access to expertise
questions.solution: Outsourcing: reduce fixed cost, access specialized skills. Risk: quality control, IP protection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Economic order quantity (EOQ) assumes:
options: Constant demand, instantaneous replenishment, no quantity discounts
questions.solution: EOQ: Q = √(2DS/H). Assumptions rarely hold exactly. But useful as starting point.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aggregate planning: chase strategy:
options: Match production to demand by varying workforce (hiring, firing)
questions.solution: Chase: varies output rate. Hiring/firing costs. Low inventory. Opposite of level strategy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) path planning:
options: Unidirectional or bidirectional paths. Zone control or continuous path
questions.solution: AGV: wire, magnetic tape, or free-ranging (laser, vision). Traffic management required.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability: bathtub curve has three phases:
questions.solution: Bathtub: early failures (burn-in), random failures (useful life), wear-out failures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermography — emissivity correction:
options: ε = 0.95 (painted), 0.3 (polished metal). Reflected apparent temperature correction. ΔT > 10°C = fault
questions.solution: Thermography: electrical (loose connections), mechanical (bearing hot), refractory (lining wear).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV):
options: Wire/tape/laser guided. Flexible routing. Battery powered
questions.solution: AGV: unit load, towing, pallet trucks. AS/RS: automated storage/retrieval system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bias detection — runs test:
options: Number of consecutive positive/negative errors. Too few runs → bias (trend). Too many → oscillation
questions.solution: Runs test: n₁ = number of +, n₂ = number of -. E(R) = 2n₁n₂/(n₁+n₂) + 1. V(R) = 2n₁n₂(2n₁n₂-n₁-n₂)/((n₁+n₂)²(n₁+n₂-1)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehouse slotting: golden zone is:
options: Waist-to-shoulder height area for fastest-moving items
questions.solution: Golden zone: ergonomic, fastest picks. Reduces bending and reaching. Slotting by velocity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VED (Vital-Essential-Desirable):
options: By criticality. V: stockout → shutdown. E: significant impact. D: minor impact
questions.solution: VED: for spare parts. V high criticality: high safety stock. D: optional, low priority.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+8, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 3+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 13, 1-3-4 = 8. The critical path is the longest = 13 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MRP lot sizing: Silver-Meal heuristic:
options: Minimize average cost per period by varying lot size (dynamic)
questions.solution: Silver-Meal: find period T where cumulative cost/T is minimized. Order covers periods 1 through T.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Artificial variable cost in Big-M method:
options: Large penalty M to drive artificial to zero
questions.solution: Big-M: artificial variables have cost M (for minimization) or -M (for maximization). Drives to zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fatigue — rest allowance formula:
options: RA = (T - K)/K × 100%. T = max repetitive time. K = time at standard pace
questions.solution: Rest allowance: increases with heat, noise, mental load. Personal + basic fatigue + special allowance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹60/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 20000/(100 − 60) = 500 units, where total revenue = total cost.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="240" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="80" x2="240" y2="80" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="180" y="76" font-size="8" fill="#475569">total cost</text><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="220" y2="25" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="180" y="40" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">revenue</text><circle cx="120" cy="62" r="4" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="120" y1="62" x2="120" y2="120" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="110" y="134" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">BEP</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: X-bar chart: X̄=50, R̄=4, A₂=0.577. UCL≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermography for electrical inspection:
options: Hot spots indicate loose connections, overloaded circuits, failing components
questions.solution: IR thermography: detect thermal anomalies. Electrical: loose connection = hot spot. Delta T > 10C = significant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT — activity variance:
options: σ² = ((b-a)/6)². b = pessimistic, a = optimistic. β-distribution assumption
questions.solution: Variance: larger range = more uncertainty. σ = (b-a)/6 → 99.7% within ±3σ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Failure mode — Weibull analysis:
options: β < 1 (infant mortality), β = 1 (random failure), β > 1 (wear-out). MTBF from Weibull parameters η, β
questions.solution: Weibull: reliability R(t) = exp(-(t/η)^β). Hazard: h(t) = (β/η)(t/η)^{β-1}. Probability plot.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 20/hour and spend on average 0.5 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:
questions.solution: Little's law: L = λ·W = 20×0.5 = 10.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Availability = MTBF/(MTBF+MTTR). For MTBF=100h, MTTR=10h:
questions.solution: Availability: proportion of time system is operational. Higher MTBF or lower MTTR improves availability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LP — Two-phase method:
options: Phase 1: minimize sum of artificials. Phase 2: original objective from feasible basis
questions.solution: Phase 1: if min = 0 → feasible. Phase 2: drop artificials, original objective.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assignment — unbalanced:
options: Number of rows ≠ columns. Add dummy rows/cols with zero cost
questions.solution: Unbalanced: dummy added. No matching for dummy. May be degenerate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Series reliability: R₁=0.95, R₂=0.95. R_series=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assignment problem: Hungarian method for maximization:
options: Convert to minimization by subtracting all elements from maximum element in matrix
questions.solution: Max → min: new c_ij = max(c) - c_ij. Then apply Hungarian. Optimal assignment same.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Condition monitoring: vibration analysis detects:
options: Imbalance, misalignment, bearing defects, gear faults
questions.solution: Vibration: FFT spectrum. 1×=imbalance, 2×=misalignment, bearing frequencies (BPFO, BPFI).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delphi method:
options: Expert panel, anonymous rounds, facilitator. Convergence of opinion
questions.solution: Delphi: structured communication. Rounds until consensus. No dominance by single expert.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ABC — classification criteria:
options: A: high value (70-80% of total value, 10-15% items). C: low value (5%, 50-70% items)
questions.solution: ABC: inventory stratification. A: cycle count frequently. C: periodic review. Pareto principle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CMMS:
options: Computerized Maintenance Management System. Work orders, asset history, spares inventory
questions.solution: CMMS: schedule PMs. Track costs. Analyze failure trends. Interface with ERP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Critical chain project management (CCPM):
options: Remove safety from each activity → project buffer. Feeding buffers for non-critical paths. Goldratt's theory
questions.solution: CCMP: 50% duration estimates. Resource leveling first. Buffer management: green/yellow/red. Fever chart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reducing an activity's duration by adding resources, at extra cost, is called:
questions.solution: Crashing shortens critical activities at the least incremental cost to compress the schedule.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 400 units using 50 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:
questions.solution: Productivity = output/input = 400/50 = 8 units/hour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System):
options: Tracks work orders, asset history, maintenance schedules, spare parts inventory
questions.solution: CMMS: EAM (Enterprise Asset Management). History → analysis → optimization. Integration with ERP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 4000/yr, order cost ₹50 and order quantity 200, the annual ordering cost is:
questions.solution: Annual ordering cost = (D/Q)×S = (4000/200)×50 = ₹1000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Periodic review (P-system):
options: Order up to target level every P periods. Larger safety stock than Q-system
questions.solution: P-system: order quantities vary. Count at review time. No perpetual inventory count needed.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Tracking signal detects:
questions.solution: Tracking: running sum of errors / MAD.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inventory costs:
questions.solution: Holding: storage, insurance, obsolescence, capital cost. Ordering: paperwork, transport.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.3, last actual = 200 and last forecast = 280, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: MAPE:
questions.solution: MAPE = (1/n)*Sum|e_t/Y_t|*100%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARIMA (Box-Jenkins):
options: AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average. p,d,q parameters. Stationarity required
questions.solution: ARIMA: AR(p) + differencing(d) + MA(q). Identification → estimation → diagnostic checking. ACF and PACF.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Causal forecasting — regression:
options: y = a + bx. Dependent vs independent variable. R² = goodness of fit
questions.solution: Regression: least squares. b = Σ(x-x̄)(y-ȳ)/Σ(x-x̄)². y = a + bx for prediction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT vs CPM:
options: PERT: probabilistic, non-repetitive (R&D). CPM: deterministic, repetitive (construction)
questions.solution: PERT: event-oriented. CPM: activity-oriented. Both: identify critical path.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assignment problem: number of assignments = number of rows (one-to-one):
questions.solution: Assignment: n x n matrix. One job per machine. Hungarian method finds optimal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing theory analyses systems characterised by:
questions.solution: Queuing models study arrival/service rates to predict waiting times and queue lengths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The feasible region of a linear program is always:
questions.solution: Intersection of half-spaces is convex, so the LP optimum lies at a vertex of the convex feasible region.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: ABC inventory analysis classifies items by:
questions.solution: ABC: A=high value (80/20), B=medium, C=low value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The break-even point is the output level at which:
questions.solution: At break-even, contribution covers fixed cost exactly, so profit is zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Agile project management — Scrum:
options: Sprint (2-4 week), product backlog, sprint backlog, daily standup, review, retrospective. Velocity
questions.solution: Scrum: self-organizing team. PO (backlog), SM (process), Team (do the work). Burndown chart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.95 and 0.9 are in series. The system reliability is:
questions.solution: Series: Rs = R₁×R₂ = 0.95×0.9 = 0.86.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasonal decomposition: multiplicative model:
options: Y = T * S * C * I (Trend x Seasonal x Cyclical x Irregular)
questions.solution: Multiplicative: seasonal component proportional to trend. Additive: seasonal constant regardless of trend.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 6 min at a performance rating of 100%. The normal time is:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed × rating = 6×100% = 6 min.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Throughput Accounting: inventory is:
options: Money invested in things not yet sold
questions.solution: Inventory = all money in system (RM, WIP, FG, equipment). Goal: minimize inventory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Winters' method (triple exponential smoothing):
options: Level + trend + seasonality. Additive or multiplicative seasonality

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Break-even: Fixed cost=$50000, variable cost=$50/unit, selling price=$80/unit. BEP≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-period — overage cost Co:
options: Loss per unit over-ordered. c-s (cost - salvage value)
questions.solution: Newsvendor: if Cu > Co → SL > 0.5. Perishable goods, seasonal fashion, holiday items.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Throughput of a manufacturing cell depends on:
questions.solution: Cell throughput = capacity of bottleneck. Theory of Constraints. All work up to bottleneck pace.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NPV: Initial cost=$50000, annual benefit=$15000, r=12%, n=5 years. NPV≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — R chart:
options: Monitors process variability (range within samples). Centerline = average range
questions.solution: R chart: UCL = D₄·R̄, LCL = D₃·R̄. Before X-bar: check R-chart for stability.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Effective capacity is:
questions.solution: Effective capacity = design capacity × factors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EOQ: D=8000/yr, S=$40, H=$4/unit/yr. Q*=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hungarian method steps:
options: Row reduction, column reduction, line covering, augmentation
questions.solution: Hungarian: O(n³). Minimization. For maximization: negate costs or subtract from max.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kaizen:
options: Continuous incremental improvement. Small changes, everyone involved
questions.solution: Kaizen: 5S (Sort, Set, Shine, Standardize, Sustain). Poka-yoke (mistake proofing).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 3+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 10, 1-3-4 = 8. The critical path is the longest = 10 days.
